verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that when Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, so that he could not see, he called Esau his eldest son, and said unto him, My son: and he said unto him, Behold, here am I.
verses.ct: When Isaac had grown old and his eyesight had failed so that he could no longer see, he called for his older son Esau and said, "My son." Esau replied, "I'm here."
verses.web: When Isaac was old, and his eyes were dim, so that he could not see, he called Esau his elder son, and said to him, “My son?” He said to him, “Here I am.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Behold now, I am old, I know not the day of my death:
verses.ct: Isaac said, "Look, I have become old. I don't know when I will die.
verses.web: He said, “See now, I am old. I don’t know the day of my death.

verses.kjv: Now therefore take, I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow, and go out to the field, and take me some venison;
verses.ct: "So please, get your hunting gear — your quiver and your bow — and go out into the open country and hunt some wild game for me.
verses.web: Now therefore, please take your weapons, your quiver and your bow, and go out to the field, and get me venison.

verses.kjv: And make me savoury meat, such as I love, and bring it to me, that I may eat; that my soul may bless thee before I die.
verses.ct: "Then prepare a delicious meal for me, the kind I enjoy, and bring it to me so I can eat, and my soul may bless you before I die."
verses.web: Make me savory food, such as I love, and bring it to me, that I may eat, and that my soul may bless you before I die.”

verses.kjv: And Rebekah heard when Isaac spake to Esau his son. And Esau went to the field to hunt for venison, and to bring it.
verses.ct: Rebekah was listening while Isaac spoke to his son Esau. So Esau left for the open country to hunt wild game and bring it back.
verses.web: Rebekah heard when Isaac spoke to Esau his son. Esau went to the field to hunt for venison, and to bring it.

verses.kjv: And Rebekah spake unto Jacob her son, saying, Behold, I heard thy father speak unto Esau thy brother, saying,
verses.ct: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, "Listen, I overheard your father talking to your brother Esau, saying,
verses.web: Rebekah spoke to Jacob her son, saying, “Behold, I heard your father speak to Esau your brother, saying,

verses.kjv: Bring me venison, and make me savoury meat, that I may eat, and bless thee before the LORD before my death.
verses.ct: "'Bring me wild game and prepare a delicious meal for me to eat, so that I may bless you in the presence of the LORD before I die.'
verses.web: ‘Bring me venison, and make me savory food, that I may eat, and bless you before LORD before my death.’

verses.kjv: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.
verses.ct: "Now, my son, listen carefully and do exactly what I tell you.
verses.web: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command you.

verses.kjv: Go now to the flock, and fetch me from thence two good kids of the goats; and I will make them savoury meat for thy father, such as he loveth:
verses.ct: "Go to the flock and bring me two choice young goats. I will prepare them into a delicious meal for your father, just the way he loves it.
verses.web: Go now to the flock and get me two good young goats from there. I will make them savory food for your father, such as he loves.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt bring it to thy father, that he may eat, and that he may bless thee before his death.
verses.ct: "Then you will take it to your father so he can eat, and so he will bless you before he dies."
verses.web: You shall bring it to your father, that he may eat, so that he may bless you before his death.”

verses.kjv: And Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man:
verses.ct: Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, "But Esau my brother has a lot of hair on his body, and my skin is smooth.
verses.web: Jacob said to Rebekah his mother, “Behold, Esau my brother is a hairy man, and I am a smooth man.

verses.kjv: My father peradventure will feel me, and I shall seem to him as a deceiver; and I shall bring a curse upon me, and not a blessing.
verses.ct: "What if my father touches me? He would realize I'm trying to trick him, and I would bring a curse on myself instead of a blessing."
verses.web: What if my father touches me? I will seem to him as a deceiver, and I would bring a curse on myself, and not a blessing.”

verses.kjv: And his mother said unto him, Upon me be thy curse, my son: only obey my voice, and go fetch me them.
verses.ct: His mother replied, "Let any curse fall on me, my son. Just do what I say and go get the goats for me."
verses.web: His mother said to him, “Let your curse be on me, my son. Only obey my voice, and go get them for me.”

verses.kjv: And he went, and fetched, and brought them to his mother: and his mother made savoury meat, such as his father loved.
verses.ct: So he went and got them and brought them to his mother, and she prepared a delicious meal, just the way his father loved it.
verses.web: He went, and got them, and brought them to his mother. His mother made savory food, such as his father loved.

verses.kjv: And Rebekah took goodly raiment of her eldest son Esau, which were with her in the house, and put them upon Jacob her younger son:
verses.ct: Then Rebekah took the finest clothes belonging to her older son Esau, which she had in the house, and dressed her younger son Jacob in them.
verses.web: Rebekah took the good clothes of Esau, her elder son, which were with her in the house, and put them on Jacob, her younger son.

verses.kjv: And she put the skins of the kids of the goats upon his hands, and upon the smooth of his neck:
verses.ct: She also covered his hands and the smooth part of his neck with the goat skins.
verses.web: She put the skins of the young goats on his hands, and on the smooth of his neck.

verses.kjv: And she gave the savoury meat and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.
verses.ct: Then she handed the delicious meal and the bread she had prepared to her son Jacob.
verses.web: She gave the savory food and the bread, which she had prepared, into the hand of her son Jacob.

verses.kjv: And he came unto his father, and said, My father: and he said, Here am I; who art thou, my son?
verses.ct: He went to his father and said, "My father." Isaac answered, "Yes, I'm here. Which of my sons are you?"
verses.web: He came to his father, and said, “My father?” He said, “Here I am. Who are you, my son?”

verses.kjv: And Jacob said unto his father, I am Esau thy first born; I have done according as thou badest me: arise, I pray thee, sit and eat of my venison, that thy soul may bless me.
verses.ct: Jacob told his father, "I am Esau, your firstborn. I have done what you asked. Please, sit up and eat some of my wild game so that your soul may bless me."
verses.web: Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau your firstborn. I have done what you asked me to do. Please arise, sit and eat of my venison, that your soul may bless me.”

verses.kjv: And Isaac said unto his son, How is it that thou hast found it so quickly, my son? And he said, Because the LORD thy God brought it to me.
verses.ct: Isaac asked his son, "How did you find it so fast, my son?" He answered, "Because the LORD your God led it right to me."
verses.web: Isaac said to his son, “How is it that you have found it so quickly, my son?” He said, “Because LORD your God gave me success.”

verses.kjv: And Isaac said unto Jacob, Come near, I pray thee, that I may feel thee, my son, whether thou be my very son Esau or not.
verses.ct: Then Isaac said to Jacob, "Come closer, please, so I can touch you, my son, and know whether you really are my son Esau or not."
verses.web: Isaac said to Jacob, “Please come near, that I may feel you, my son, whether you are really my son Esau or not.”

verses.kjv: And Jacob went near unto Isaac his father; and he felt him, and said, The voice is Jacob’s voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.
verses.ct: Jacob moved closer to his father Isaac, who touched him and said, "The voice sounds like Jacob's voice, but the hands feel like Esau's hands."
verses.web: Jacob went near to Isaac his father. He felt him, and said, “The voice is Jacob’s voice, but the hands are the hands of Esau.”

verses.kjv: And he discerned him not, because his hands were hairy, as his brother Esau’s hands: so he blessed him.
verses.ct: He did not recognize him because his hands were hairy like his brother Esau's hands. So he blessed him.
verses.web: He didn’t recognize him, because his hands were hairy, like his brother, Esau’s hands. So he blessed him.

verses.kjv: And he said, Art thou my very son Esau? And he said, I am.
verses.ct: He asked again, "Are you really my son Esau?" Jacob answered, "I am."
verses.web: He said, “Are you really my son Esau?” He said, “I am.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son’s venison, that my soul may bless thee. And he brought it near to him, and he did eat: and he brought him wine, and he drank.
verses.ct: Isaac said, "Serve it to me, and let me eat my son's wild game so that my soul may bless you." Jacob brought it close, and Isaac ate. He also brought him wine, and he drank.
verses.web: He said, “Bring it near to me, and I will eat of my son’s venison, that my soul may bless you.” He brought it near to him, and he ate. He brought him wine, and he drank.

verses.kjv: And his father Isaac said unto him, Come near now, and kiss me, my son.
verses.ct: Then his father Isaac said to him, "Come close now and kiss me, my son."
verses.web: His father Isaac said to him, “Come near now, and kiss me, my son.”

verses.kjv: And he came near, and kissed him: and he smelled the smell of his raiment, and blessed him, and said, See, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field which the LORD hath blessed:
verses.ct: So he came close and kissed him. When Isaac caught the scent of his clothes, he blessed him and said, "Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field that the LORD has blessed.
verses.web: He came near, and kissed him. He smelled the smell of his clothing, and blessed him, and said, “Behold, the smell of my son is as the smell of a field which LORD has blessed.

verses.kjv: Therefore God give thee of the dew of heaven, and the fatness of the earth, and plenty of corn and wine:
verses.ct: "May God give you the dew of heaven and the richness of the earth, and an abundance of grain and wine.
verses.web: God give you of the dew of the sky, of the fatness of the earth, and plenty of grain and new wine.

verses.kjv: Let people serve thee, and nations bow down to thee: be lord over thy brethren, and let thy mother’s sons bow down to thee: cursed be every one that curseth thee, and blessed be he that blesseth thee.
verses.ct: "May peoples serve you and nations bow down before you. Be lord over your brothers, and may your mother's sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you, and blessed be everyone who blesses you."
verses.web: Let peoples serve you, and nations bow down to you. Be lord over your brothers. Let your mother’s sons bow down to you. Cursed be everyone who curses you. Blessed be everyone who blesses you.”

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as soon as Isaac had made an end of blessing Jacob, and Jacob was yet scarce gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, that Esau his brother came in from his hunting.
verses.ct: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had barely left his father Isaac's presence, his brother Esau returned from his hunt.
verses.web: As soon as Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and Jacob had just gone out from the presence of Isaac his father, Esau his brother came in from his hunting.

verses.kjv: And he also had made savoury meat, and brought it unto his father, and said unto his father, Let my father arise, and eat of his son’s venison, that thy soul may bless me.
verses.ct: He too prepared a delicious meal and brought it to his father. He said to his father, "Father, please sit up and eat some of your son's wild game, so that your soul may bless me."
verses.web: He also made savory food, and brought it to his father. He said to his father, “Let my father arise, and eat of his son’s venison, that your soul may bless me.”

verses.kjv: And Isaac his father said unto him, Who art thou? And he said, I am thy son, thy firstborn Esau.
verses.ct: His father Isaac asked him, "Who are you?" He replied, "I am your son, your firstborn, Esau."
verses.web: Isaac his father said to him, “Who are you?” He said, “I am your son, your firstborn, Esau.”

verses.kjv: And Isaac trembled very exceedingly, and said, Who? where is he that hath taken venison, and brought it me, and I have eaten of all before thou camest, and have blessed him? yea, and he shall be blessed.
verses.ct: Isaac began to tremble violently and said, "Then who was it that hunted wild game and brought it to me? I ate it all before you arrived and blessed him — and indeed, he will remain blessed."
verses.web: Isaac trembled violently, and said, “Who, then, is he who has taken venison, and brought it to me, and I have eaten of all before you came, and have blessed him? Yes, he will be blessed.”

verses.kjv: And when Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with a great and exceeding bitter cry, and said unto his father, Bless me, even me also, O my father.
verses.ct: When Esau heard his father's words, he burst out with a loud and extremely bitter cry and said to his father, "Bless me — bless me too, my father!"
verses.web: When Esau heard the words of his father, he cried with an exceedingly great and bitter cry, and said to his father, “Bless me, even me also, my father.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Thy brother came with subtilty, and hath taken away thy blessing.
verses.ct: Isaac replied, "Your brother came with deceit and has taken your blessing."
verses.web: He said, “Your brother came with deceit, and has taken away your blessing.”

verses.kjv: And he said, Is not he rightly named Jacob? for he hath supplanted me these two times: he took away my birthright; and, behold, now he hath taken away my blessing. And he said, Hast thou not reserved a blessing for me?
verses.ct: Esau said, "Isn't his name rightly called Jacob? He has cheated me twice now: he took my birthright, and now look, he has taken my blessing." Then he asked, "Haven't you kept back even one blessing for me?"
verses.web: He said, “Isn’t he rightly named Jacob? For he has supplanted me these two times. He took away my birthright. See, now he has taken away my blessing.” He said, “Haven’t you reserved a blessing for me?”

verses.kjv: And Isaac answered and said unto Esau, Behold, I have made him thy lord, and all his brethren have I given to him for servants; and with corn and wine have I sustained him: and what shall I do now unto thee, my son?
verses.ct: Isaac answered Esau, "Look, I have made him lord over you and given him all his brothers as servants. I have provided him with grain and wine. What is left that I can do for you, my son?"
verses.web: Isaac answered Esau, “Behold, I have made him your lord, and all his brothers I have given to him for servants. I have sustained him with grain and new wine. What then will I do for you, my son?”

verses.kjv: And Esau said unto his father, Hast thou but one blessing, my father? bless me, even me also, O my father. And Esau lifted up his voice, and wept.
verses.ct: Esau said to his father, "Do you only have one blessing, my father? Bless me — bless me too, my father!" Then Esau raised his voice and wept.
verses.web: Esau said to his father, “Do you have just one blessing, my father? Bless me, even me also, my father.” Esau lifted up his voice, and wept.

verses.kjv: And Isaac his father answered and said unto him, Behold, thy dwelling shall be the fatness of the earth, and of the dew of heaven from above;
verses.ct: His father Isaac answered him, "Look, your dwelling will be away from the richness of the earth, and away from the dew of heaven above.
verses.web: Isaac his father answered him, “Behold, your dwelling will be of the fatness of the earth, and of the dew of the sky from above.

verses.kjv: And by thy sword shalt thou live, and shalt serve thy brother; and it shall come to pass when thou shalt have the dominion, that thou shalt break his yoke from off thy neck.
verses.ct: "You will live by your sword, and you will serve your brother. But when you grow restless, you will tear his yoke from your neck."
verses.web: You will live by your sword, and you will serve your brother. It will happen, when you will break loose, that you will shake his yoke from off your neck.”

verses.kjv: And Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing wherewith his father blessed him: and Esau said in his heart, The days of mourning for my father are at hand; then will I slay my brother Jacob.
verses.ct: Esau held a deep grudge against Jacob because of the blessing his father had given him. Esau said to himself, "The time to mourn my father's death is near; after that, I will kill my brother Jacob."
verses.web: Esau hated Jacob because of the blessing with which his father blessed him. Esau said in his heart, “The days of mourning for my father are at hand. Then I will kill my brother Jacob.”

verses.kjv: And these words of Esau her elder son were told to Rebekah: and she sent and called Jacob her younger son, and said unto him, Behold, thy brother Esau, as touching thee, doth comfort himself, purposing to kill thee.
verses.ct: When Rebekah was told what her older son Esau had said, she sent for her younger son Jacob and told him, "Listen, your brother Esau is consoling himself with plans to kill you.
verses.web: The words of Esau, her elder son, were told to Rebekah. She sent and called Jacob, her younger son, and said to him, “Behold, your brother Esau comforts himself about you by planning to kill you.

verses.kjv: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice; and arise, flee thou to Laban my brother to Haran;
verses.ct: "So now, my son, do what I say: get up and escape to my brother Laban in Haran.
verses.web: Now therefore, my son, obey my voice. Arise, flee to Laban, my brother, in Haran.

verses.kjv: And tarry with him a few days, until thy brother’s fury turn away;
verses.ct: "Stay with him for a while, until your brother's rage subsides —
verses.web: Stay with him a few days, until your brother’s fury turns away—

verses.kjv: Until thy brother’s anger turn away from thee, and he forget that which thou hast done to him: then I will send, and fetch thee from thence: why should I be deprived also of you both in one day?
verses.ct: "until your brother's anger toward you fades and he forgets what you did to him. Then I will send word and bring you back from there. Why should I lose both of you in a single day?"
verses.web: until your brother’s anger turns away from you, and he forgets what you have done to him. Then I will send, and get you from there. Why should I be bereaved of you both in one day?”

verses.kjv: And Rebekah said to Isaac, I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth: if Jacob take a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these which are of the daughters of the land, what good shall my life do me?
verses.ct: Then Rebekah said to Isaac, "I am sick of living because of these Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women from this land, like these local girls, what reason would I have to go on living?"
verses.web: Rebekah said to Isaac, “I am weary of my life because of the daughters of Heth. If Jacob takes a wife of the daughters of Heth, such as these, of the daughters of the land, what good will my life do me?”
